title: Quadratic Equations
subject: math
audioScript: Good morning, everyone. Today we're opening Quadratic Equations. Forget the heavy textbook tone for a moment — think of this chapter as a story about Standard form, Factorization method, and Quadratic formula. I'll use everyday examples first, then we'll connect them to what CBSE expects in the exam.

segments.teleprompter: Good morning, everyone. Today we're opening Quadratic Equations. Forget the heavy textbook tone for a moment — think of this chapter as a story about Standard form, Factorization method, and Quadratic formula. I'll use everyday examples first, then we'll connect them to what CBSE expects in the exam.

Before you press play on any video about Quadratic Equations, grab a notebook. Pause every two minutes and write one sentence in your own words. The links here are curated for Class 10 CBSE — NCERT-style explainers and one-shot revision.

Imagine I'm sitting beside you before the board exam. Here's Quadratic Equations the way I'd explain it to a student who said 'I read it but didn't get it.' Slow pace, simple words, one idea at a time.
Quick recap of Quadratic Equations. Three things to remember tonight: the big idea, one formula or rule, and one mistake to avoid. That's enough for a confident revision.

That is, ax2+ bx + c = 0, a ≠ 0 is called the standard form of a quadratic equation.

sections.title: 2. Key Definitions
sections.body:
**Key definitions:**

- NCERT Solutions Class 10 Maths Chapter 4 Quadratic Equations contain all the solutions to the problems provided in the Class 10 Maths NCERT textbook for CBSE exam preparations. The questions from every section are framed and solved accurately by the subject experts. NCERT Solutions for Class 10 are detailed and step-by-step guides to all the queries of the students. The exercises present in the chapter should be dealt with utmost sincerity if one wants to score well in the examinations. Maths is a subject that requires a good understanding and a lot of practice. The tips and tricks to solve the problems easily are also provided here. A quadratic equation in the variable x is an equation of the form ax2+ bx + c = 0, where a, b, c are real numbers, a ≠ 0. That is, ax2+ bx + c = 0, a ≠ 0 is called the standard form of a quadratic equation.
- Quadratic equations arise in several situations around us. Hence, students should give special attention to learning the concepts related to this chapter of the latest CBSE Syllabus for 2023-24 thoroughly to excel in Class 10 Maths examinations. NCERT Solutions help the students in learning these concepts as well as in evaluating themselves. Practising these solutions repeatedly is bound to help the students in overcoming their shortcomings. Maths has either a correct answer or a wrong one. Therefore, it is imperative to concentrate while solving the questions to score full marks.
- 1. Check whether the following are quadratic equations:

- Therefore, the given equation is a quadratic equation.
- The above equation is not in the form of ax2 + bx + c = 0
- Therefore, the given equation is not a quadratic equation.

Exercise 4.1 Page: 73
1. Check whether the following are quadratic equations:
(i) (x + 1)2 = 2(x – 3)
(ii) x2 – 2x = (–2) (3 – x)
(iii) (x – 2)(x + 1) = (x – 1)(x + 3)
(iv) (x – 3)(2x +1) = x(x + 5)
(v) (2x – 1)(x – 3) = (x + 5)(x – 1)
(vi) x2 + 3x + 1 = (x – 2)2
(vii) (x + 2)3 = 2x (x2 – 1)
(viii) x3 – 4x2 – x + 1 = (x – 2)3
Solutions:
(i) Given,
(x + 1)2 = 2(x – 3)
By using the formula for (a+b)2 = a2+2ab+b2
⇒ x2 + 2x +…

sections.title: 4. Formulas / Theorems
sections.body:
**Formulas and relations:**

- NCERT Solutions Class 10 Maths Chapter 4 Quadratic Equations contain all the solutions to the problems provided in the Class 10 Maths NCERT textbook for CBSE exam preparations. The questions from every section are framed and solved accurately by the subject experts. NCERT Solutions for Class 10 are detailed and step-by-step guides to all the queries of the students. The exercises present in the chapter should be dealt with utmost sincerity if one wants to score well in the examinations. Maths is a subject that requires a good understanding and a lot of practice. The tips and tricks to solve the problems easily are also provided here. A quadratic equation in the variable x is an equation of the form ax2+ bx + c = 0, where a, b, c are real numbers, a ≠ 0. That is, ax2+ bx + c = 0, a ≠ 0 is called the standard form of a quadratic equation.
- (i) (x + 1)2 = 2(x – 3)
- (ii) x2 – 2x = (–2) (3 – x)
- (iii) (x – 2)(x + 1) = (x – 1)(x + 3)
- (iv) (x – 3)(2x +1) = x(x + 5)
- (v) (2x – 1)(x – 3) = (x + 5)(x – 1)
- (vi) x2 + 3x + 1 = (x – 2)2
- (vii) (x + 2)3 = 2x (x2 – 1)

sections.title: 11. Video Transcript Summary
sections.body:
_Teacher recap — what a strong Class 10 video lesson on **Quadratic Equations** should cover:_

**Lesson flow (as if narrated on screen):**

1. **Hook** — Start with a daily-life example tied to Quadratic Equations, not a textbook definition.
2. **Standard form** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
3. **Factorization method** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
4. **Quadratic formula** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
5. **Nature of roots** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
6. **Formulas** — Write ax^2 + bx + c = 0 on the board, explain each symbol, then solve one board-style numerical.
7. **Common mistake** — Warn about the error students make most often in exams.
8. **Recap** — Three bullet points to remember before moving to practice questions.

**After watching, you should be able to:**

- Explain Standard form in everyday language.
- Draw or describe one diagram related to Quadratic Equations.
- Attempt at least two short-answer questions without notes.

**Videos linked for this chapter:**

- 🔍 [CBSE class 10 Quadratic Equations NCERT explained Hindi English](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=CBSE+class+10+Quadratic+Equations+NCERT+explained+Hindi+English)
- 🔍 [Quadratic Equations class 10 one shot revision](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=Quadratic+Equations+class+10+one+shot+revision)
- 🔍 [NCERT Mathematics class 10 Quadratic Equations animation](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=NCERT+Mathematics+class+10+Quadratic+Equations+animation)
